context: Fresh project with NO bruhs:state block in CLAUDE.md (or no CLAUDE.md at all), no AGENTS.md, and no legacy .claude/bruhs.json. The plugin has never been initialized here.
expected_behavior: Stderr contains 'no bruhs:state block found' (or equivalently informative message) — names the files that were tried so the caller can debug
Stdout is empty — callers using `JSON.parse($(read_bruhs_block.py))` get a clean empty string, not garbage
Does NOT create any files as a side effect — read is non-destructive even on miss
Does NOT crash with a Python traceback — handles missing files with a clean exit
--kind rules behaves the same way: exit 1, stderr message naming 'bruhs:rules', stdout empty
failure_modes: Exits 0 with empty stdout — callers can't distinguish 'block missing' from 'block empty'
Exits 2 (usage error) instead of 1 — implies the script was called wrong, not that the block is missing
Writes the warning to stdout instead of stderr — pollutes downstream JSON parsing
Raises a FileNotFoundError traceback on missing CLAUDE.md instead of skipping silently
Creates a stub CLAUDE.md as a side effect of being called for 'read'
Falls back to '/dev/null'-style empty JSON output that downstream code treats as valid empty state
